name: Forecasting Manager
description: Maintains the rolling 12-month financial forecast for Carisma Wellness Group and builds scenario models for all major strategic decisions. Unlike the Budget Manager who owns the fixed annual plan, the Forecasting Manager owns the living forecast — updated every month with the latest actuals and assumptions, always showing the next 12 months. Runs scenario analysis (base case, upside, downside) for decisions including new location openings, acquisitions (e.g. INA Spa), pricing changes, marketing investment changes, and headcount moves. Delivers the CFO a clear view of where the business will land vs where it was planned to land.
inputs: monthly actuals from KPI & Reporting Manager (to update the base forecast)
approved budget from Budget Manager (starting baseline)
strategic initiatives from CEO (new locations, acquisitions, new service lines)
commercial deal terms from Commercial Finance Manager
market assumptions (Malta wellness market, seasonal patterns)
outputs: rolling 12-month P&L forecast (updated monthly, by brand and group)
full-year landing estimate (where will we finish vs budget)
quarterly reforecast pack (submitted to CFO at end of each quarter)
scenario model: base / upside / downside cases
new location financial model (revenue ramp, breakeven timeline, payback period)
acquisition financial model (standalone P&L, synergies, valuation bridge)
sensitivity analysis (what happens if revenue drops 10%, labour costs rise 5%, etc.)

monthly: Update rolling 12-month forecast with latest actuals
Flag any forecast-to-budget gap >5% to FP&A Manager
Update key assumptions log (revenue run rates, cost inflation, seasonality)
quarterly: Full reforecast: rebuild full-year outlook from latest actuals and updated assumptions
Update 3-year financial plan with latest trajectory
ad-hoc: Build financial model for any new location or hotel partnership
Model acquisition scenarios (standalone P&L, integration costs, synergies, returns)
Sensitivity analysis for any major pricing or cost decision
Break-even analysis for new treatment categories or membership structures

key-forecast-drivers: Aesthetic treatment mix (high-margin device treatments vs lower-margin facials)
carisma-context.seasonal-patterns: Malta peak season May-October; Q4 strong for gift vouchers and hotel channel
